Study_population: Ugandan population (continental African ancestry); T2D/prediabetes + normoglycemic controls
snippet: We link genome-wide data to protein expression levels and construct a protein quantitative trait locus (pQTL) map for this population. We identify 399 independent associations with 346 (86.7%) cis-pQTLs and 53 (13.3%) trans-pQTLs.
notes: De-novo proteomic measurement in a continental-African cohort + first pQTL map; downstream coloc/causal analysis is secondary.

snippet: we integrated serum measurements of 7,596 proteins with genomic data, including LF variants (minor allele frequency [MAF] 0.1-1%), in 5,291 Icelanders to identify independent cis-pQTLs for 2,166 SOMAmers.
notes: De-novo SomaScan serum measurement + cis-pQTL discovery focused on low-frequency variants; preprint.

Study_population: Women's Health Initiative + Framingham Heart Study (1033 African American, 1764 non-Hispanic White); replication in Jackson Heart Study (534 AA)
snippet: We conduct an Olink protein quantitative trait locus (pQTL) analysis on 1245 proteins involving 1033 self-identified African American (AA) and 1764 non-Hispanic White (NHW) participants from the Women's Health Initiative and Framingham Heart Study.
notes: De-novo Olink pQTL discovery in diverse ancestries; 195 previously unreported pQTLs, mostly African-ancestry-specific. UKB-PPP used only for replication. Check overlap with prior FHS Olink pQTL work.

Reference: Stinson et al., Am J Hum Genet 2025
snippet: We analyzed genotypes and plasma proteomics data (Olink Target 96 Inflammation and Cardiovascular II panels) from 3,707 Greenlandic individuals ... Among 177 proteins, we identified 251 primary pQTLs.
notes: Clean de-novo pQTL discovery in an underrepresented population; novel cis-pQTL for IL-6 reported.

snippet: Using the Seer Proteograph nanoparticle enrichment MS platform, we identified and replicated new pQTLs in a genome-wide association study of proteins in blood plasma samples from two cohorts.
notes: De-novo MS proteomics + new genome-wide pQTL discovery and replication; also evaluates affinity-platform pQTLs but has primary scan. · folds preprint/twin: 10.1101/2024.05.27.596028

snippet: We measured plasma levels of 2168 proteins in 3976 Chinese adults using both Olink Explore and SomaScan platforms ... 765 Olink and 513 SomaScan proteins had cis-pQTLs, including 400 with colocalising cis-pQTLs.
notes: De-novo measurement on both platforms in Chinese cohort with own cis-pQTL discovery; cross-platform comparison. · folds preprint/twin: 10.1101/2023.12.01.23299236

Platform: NR (proteomics platform not stated in abstract)
snippet: we generated and integrated plasma proteomics and metabolomics with the genotype datasets of over 2300 European (EUR) and 400 African (AFR) ancestries... we mapped 954 AFR pQTLs, 2848 EUR pQTLs.
notes: De-novo plasma proteomics + ancestry-specific pQTL discovery; also mQTLs and downstream INTACT/coloc for T2D. Platform not named in abstract.

Study_population: 1,362 brain donors (African American, Hispanic/Latin American, non-Hispanic white)
snippet: we mapped protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) in 1,362 brain proteomes from African American, Hispanic/Latin American and non-Hispanic white donors.
notes: De-novo multiancestry brain pQTL mapping with fine-mapping; MR is downstream (has primary data). Platform inferred as MS; not 100% explicit in abstract.

Platform: Proteograph XT (Seer) nanoparticle MS (also SomaLogic 11k, Olink HT in same samples)
snippet: Here, we integrate exome sequencing and common non-coding variation with untargeted nanoparticle enriched mass spectrometry (MS)-based plasma proteomics (Seer Proteograph XT: n=8067 protein groups) in >1,400 British-Bangladeshi and British-Pakistani individuals.
notes: De-novo MS proteomics + new pQTL discovery in South Asian cohort; cross-platform comparison with SomaScan/Olink; preprint (no PMID).

Sample_type: Plasma + tissue (aortic wall, mammary artery, liver, skeletal muscle)
snippet: We employed the Olink Explore 3.2k platform to assess relative protein levels in each tissue. We identify 608 cis-pQTLs, the majority of which are found in plasma ... 13 proteins with exclusive tissue-specific pQTLs.
notes: De-novo multi-tissue Olink measurement in STARNET with derivation of tissue-specific cis-pQTLs; clean primary multi-tissue pGWAS (also runs coloc/MR downstream).

snippet: Here, we mapped pQTLs for 178 plasma proteins measured using affinity-based proteomics in 3,853 Danish children and adolescents... we identified 1,328 independent, genome-wide significant associations for 178 proteins.
notes: De-novo affinity proteomic measurement + genome-wide pQTL discovery in a pediatric cohort (novel age group); also runs one-sample MR; preprint (no PMID).

snippet: we used mass spectrometry to profile CSF proteins in 1,005 participants measuring 2,961 proteins... We identified 1,417 cis and 130 trans pQTLs, and uncover 24 candidate causal proteins.
notes: De-novo mass-spec CSF proteogenomics with cis/trans-pQTL discovery; novel vs affinity-based CSF pQTL studies (1066 proteins not on other platforms). Preprint (no PMID). Integrates with AD GWAS.

snippet: We profiled 974 plasma proteins in 532 CAD-patients of the STARNET study ... 144 plasma cis-pQTLs colocalized with tissue eQTLs including eight CMD/CAD GWAS genes.
notes: De-novo plasma proteomics in STARNET with cis-pQTL discovery + tissue eQTL coloc/GRN integration. Specific platform not named in abstract; small N.

Study_population: 1930 participants (European, African, East Asian, Admixed American ancestry)
snippet: we examined 2,157 proteins measured on both SomaScan 7k and Olink Explore 3072 across 1,930 participants ... Protein measures were associated with variants (pQTLs), and one-third of the pQTL signals were driven by protein-altering variants (PAVs).
notes: De-novo dual-platform measurement with own cross-ancestry pQTL discovery and PAV analysis.

snippet: We conducted a comprehensive protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL) analysis on a unique set of Gyrus Temporalis Medius (GTM) samples... This investigation revealed 8,081 genetic variants associated with the abundance of 227 proteins.
notes: De-novo brain proteome + pQTL discovery in a unique GTM tissue set, including novel variant-protein links vs prior cortex study; preprint (no PMID). Platform_class assumed mass spec (typical for brain proteomics).

snippet: Here we report a pQTL study using the Proteograph Product Suite workflow (Seer, Inc.) where we quantify over 18,000 unique peptides from nearly 3000 proteins in more than 320 blood samples ... We identify 184 protein-altering variants in 137 genes that are significantly associated with their corresponding variant peptides.
notes: De-novo nanoparticle MS proteomics with genetic discovery of protein-altering/peptide variant pQTLs; clean primary pGWAS.

Platform: NR (proteomics platform not stated in abstract)
snippet: Here we report cis-eQTL and cis-pQTL statistical fine-mapping from 1,405 genotyped samples with blood mRNA and 2,932 plasma samples of protein expression, as part of the Japan COVID-19 Task Force (JCTF).
notes: De-novo plasma proteomics + cis/trans-pQTL discovery and fine-mapping in Japanese cohort; also reports cis-eQTL. Proteomic platform not named in abstract.

Reference: Tahir et al., J Clin Invest 2024
Study_population: Jackson Heart Study (Black/African American adults); validation in MESA (multi-ethnic)
snippet: We conducted a discovery GWAS of approximately 3,000 plasma proteins measured by the antibody-based Olink platform in 1,054 Black adults from the Jackson Heart Study (JHS) and validated our findings in the Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis (MESA)... We identified 1,002 pQTLs for 925 protein assays.
notes: De-novo Olink measurement + ancestry-enriched discovery pGWAS with fine-mapping/admixture; downstream PheWAS is secondary.

snippet: Here we generated deep human brain proteomics data by quantifying 11,608 proteins across 268 subjects using 11-plex tandem mass tag... Our analysis revealed 788 cis-acting protein quantitative trait loci.
notes: De-novo brain TMT-MS proteome + cis-pQTL discovery; also runs SMR/MR but has primary measurement and scan.

snippet: We performed sex-stratified meta-analysis after protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL) mapping using inflammatory biomarkers profiled using targeted proteomics (Olink inflammatory panel) of two population-based cohorts of Europeans.
notes: De-novo Olink pQTL mapping with sex-stratified analysis; coloc downstream. Panel size/N not stated in abstract.

Reference: Smith et al., Drug Metab Dispos 2024
Study_population: Individual human livers, multi-ethnic (Illumina MEGA genotyping)
journal: Drug metabolism and disposition: the biological fate of chemicals
snippet: we performed a genome-wide association study (GWAS) to identify novel variants associated with CYP2D6 protein expression ... A data-independent acquisition (DIA)-based proteomics method was used to quantify CYP2D6 protein concentrations.
notes: De-novo DIA-MS quantification of CYP2D6 in human livers + GWAS of protein expression; genuine de-novo pQTL discovery though single-protein/tissue focus.

Study_population: Atherosclerosis Risk in Communities (ARIC); European + African (19.8%) ancestry
N_pQTL_total: 1408 proteins with pQTL (AFR), 2565 proteins with pQTL (EUR)
snippet: Of 4,651 proteins, 1,408 and 2,565 proteins had protein-quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) identified in African and European ancestry respectively.
notes: De-novo SomaScan pQTL discovery in ARIC across two ancestries; preprint (no PMID). Likely overlaps with prior ARIC SomaScan pQTL work (e.g. Zhang et al. 2022) - flag.

Study_population: Healthy individuals alternating omnivory/dietary restriction (N=200) + omnivorous controls (N=211), Greek
snippet: Using longitudinal plasma proteomic and genotypic data, we identified diet-responsive cis-protein quantitative trait loci (cis-pQTLs) including gain of genetic regulatory effects for LBR and MSRA.
notes: De-novo longitudinal plasma proteomic + genotype data with discovery of diet/season-responsive cis-pQTLs; primary measurement and genetic mapping, though platform/N-proteins not stated and gene-by-environment focus. Keep.

N_trans_pQTL: GBA trans effects on 7 CSF proteins (DLB)
snippet: We tested to what extent effects of known dementia risk loci can be observed in CSF levels of 665 proteins [proximity extension-based (PEA) immunoassays] ... We identified four AD risk loci as protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL).
notes: De-novo CSF PEA measurement + genetic pQTL discovery (locus-targeted plus stratified), with independent replication; keep though analysis is risk-locus-focused rather than fully genome-wide.

Reference: Mazidi et al., J Am Coll Cardiol 2023
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
snippet: cis-protein quantitative loci (pQTLs) identified for proteins in genome-wide association studies of CKB and of UK Biobank were used as instrumental variables.
notes: De-novo Olink Explore measurement in CKB with own genome-wide cis-pQTL discovery; heavy MR/drug-target framing but has primary scan. UKB cis-pQTLs used for replication.

Reference: Western et al., Sci Transl Med 2026
snippet: We compared the pQTL landscapes between plasma and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) ... Of almost 5000 CSF and plasma pQTLs, fewer than 30% were present in both fluids.
notes: CSF/plasma proteogenomics deriving ~5000 pQTLs across fluids; de-novo measurement plausible but platform/N not stated in abstract; borderline per CSF-proteogenomics clause.

snippet: Here, we present the largest proteogenomic study to date (n = 78,664 participants across 38 studies), identifying >24,000 protein quantitative trait loci (QTLs) associated with 1,116 proteins.
notes: Multi-cohort meta-analysis across 38 studies (web lookup: described as multi-cohort meta-analysis, no single de-novo measurement generated by this paper); leans meta_of_existing but aggregates cohort-level pQTL data. Borderline pending whether contributing cohorts are primary vs summary-stat.

Reference: Stephan et al., J Proteome Res 2026
Study_population: German, Arab-Asian and Qatari cohorts (3 independent)
snippet: we applied highly multiplexed aptamer-based affinity proteomics to plasma samples from three independent cohorts ... to evaluate how adjusting for all measured proteins influences protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) associations.
notes: Methods/framework paper on latent preanalytical variation using de-novo aptamer data; uses pQTL p-gain as a readout rather than reporting a new genome-wide pQTL catalog; flag as method-leaning borderline.

Study_population: Jackson Heart Study (African American), N=1,989; replication MESA (N=196)
N_pQTL_total: 62 proteins associated with local African ancestry (21 independent of known GWAS variants)
snippet: We performed admixture mapping of the circulating proteome in 1,989 participants from the Jackson Heart Study (JHS)... 21 of 62 remained statistically significant after conditioning on protein-associated variants observed in previous GWASs.
notes: De-novo proteome in JHS; admixture mapping (not classical SNP pGWAS) of genetic determinants of protein levels, conditioned on prior GWAS variants - pGWAS-adjacent, borderline keep.

Reference: Maya et al., Int J Mol Sci 2026
snippet: We used protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL) analyses, adjusted for covariates using linear and logistic regression, to identify genetic variants significantly associated with plasma complement protein levels.
notes: De-novo pQTL discovery on plasma complement proteins in a custom cohort; very small N and platform not specified, candidate-pathway (not full genome-wide proteome) scope. Methodologically primary but limited.

snippet: Proteomic analyses validated functional associations of CNVs with nearby genes (cis-protein quantitative trait loci; cis-pQTLs) ... and uncovered previously unknown protein-protein interactions (trans-pQTLs).
notes: Borderline: new genome-wide CNV-pQTL discovery (cis + trans) — pGWAS-adjacent. Protein measurements reused from UKB-PPP (Olink), not measured de novo; primarily a CNV PheWAS.

Study_population: Two cohorts (UK Biobank-PPP + Fenland), 30,307 females + 26,058 males
snippet: we systematically investigated sex differences in the genetic regulation of plasma proteome (>5800 protein targets) across two cohorts ... only 103 sex-differential protein quantitative loci (sd-pQTLs; ... from antibody- and aptamer-based platforms).
notes: Reuses UKB-PPP (Olink) + Fenland (SomaScan) measurements (no de-novo measurement) but performs a novel genome-wide sex-differential pQTL scan; borderline, consistent with idx 40/52.

Study_population: UK Biobank proteomics cohort (European), N=48,594; replication in Olink and MS cohorts
snippet: Applying this method to the UK Biobank proteomics cohort of 48,594 individuals, we identify 123 proteins that are correlated with non-linear covariates and 15 with genetic dominance and epistasis.
notes: Deep-learning method (EIR-auto-GP) to detect dominance/epistasis pQTL effects; reuses UKB-PPP Olink measurements (no de-novo measurement by authors), but performs a genome-wide complex-effect pQTL scan; method-paper, borderline.

Study_population: UK Biobank Caucasian (discovery); Japanese, Finnish, South Asian, Black (replication)
snippet: We conducted a sex-stratified, genome-wide association study on 2,922 proteins using data from 30,272 individuals of Caucasian ancestry from the UK Biobank.
notes: Reuses UKB-PPP Olink measurements (not de-novo), but performs novel sex-stratified genome-wide pQTL discovery with multi-ancestry replication; also runs MR. Exclude is defensible under strict de-novo criterion.

snippet: Genome-wide association studies identified genetic loci associated with CSF to plasma ratios of 241 proteins.
notes: Borderline: de-novo SomaScan CSF/plasma measurement + genome-wide ratio-QTL (CSF:plasma ratio), an explicitly flagged borderline-keep category.

Study_population: UK Biobank (up to 109,385 with parent-of-origin inference); replication Estonian Biobank, MoBa
snippet: Genome-wide association study scans for 59 complex traits and over 14,000 protein quantitative trait loci contrasting maternal and paternal effects identified over 30 POEs.
notes: Reuses UKB-PPP protein measurements (no de-novo measurement) but performs a genuine novel parent-of-origin pQTL scan; flagged borderline as it generates new pQTL-type statistics without new proteomics.

snippet: We measured all 369 proteins in the Olink Explore 384 Cardiometabolic ... panel of 500 patients with type 2 diabetes ... Protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) were derived by coupling genomic and proteomic data.
notes: De-novo Olink measurement and own cis-pQTL derivation in 500 T2D patients; small panel/N and primarily a drug-target MR study, so borderline but has primary pQTL data.

Study_population: China Kadoorie Biobank (East Asian) + UK Biobank (European)
snippet: We extracted individual cis-protein quantitative trait locus for 2923 proteins measured using Olink Explore panel from a genome-wide association study in prospective China Kadoorie Biobank and UK Biobank.
notes: Performs own GWAS-derived cis-pQTL scan in CKB+UKB Olink data, then MR/coloc/drug-target downstream; has primary scan so keep, but main thrust is MR/druggability and CKB measurement may overlap CKB-PPP. Borderline.

snippet: The same variants were used to perform protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL) analysis in a set of 362 plasma samples profiled on the Olink platform ... We identified ... 1,273 pQTLs linked to 22 proteins.
notes: De-novo Olink measurement with own pQTL analysis, but restricted to T2D GWAS-Catalog variants (sub-genome-wide, p<=1e-4) rather than a genome-wide scan; small N. Borderline.

snippet: We systematically mapped cis-vQTLs for 2,923 plasma proteins in 52,706 UK Biobank participants ... identifying 2,162 vQTLs (P < 5x10-8) for 781 proteins.
notes: Genome-wide cis-variance-QTL (vQTL) mapping across ancestries on reused UKB-PPP Olink measurements; pGWAS-adjacent (vQTL, not mean pQTL) and no de-novo measurement, hence borderline. Overlaps UKB-PPP.

Study_population: >90,000 individuals across Olink and SomaScan cohorts
snippet: We performed a large-scale cross-platform meta-analysis of plasma proteomics, integrating data from over 90,000 individuals across Olink and SomaScan platforms... This effort identified >30,000 sentinel pQTLs.
notes: Cross-platform MTAG meta-analysis/integration of existing Olink (likely UKB-PPP) and SomaScan (likely deCODE) data plus PWAS resource; no de-novo measurement. Massive pQTL output but reuses published measurements - meta_of_existing, flagged borderline; likely overlaps UKB-PPP/deCODE.

snippet: We previously performed and identified tissue-specific protein quantitative trait loci in brain, cerebrospinal fluid, and plasma ... We identified 38 genomic regions associated with 43 proteins in brain, 150 ... in cerebrospinal fluid, and 95 ... in plasma.
notes: Atlas/imputation update (TOPMed) of a previously measured SomaScan proteome rather than new de-novo measurement; performs a real multi-tissue pQTL scan with novel loci, hence borderline.

snippet: we examined the effects of HLA genetic variation on the expression of 2940 plasma proteins across 45,330 Europeans in the UK Biobank ... We detected 504 proteins affected by HLA variants (HLA-pQTL).
notes: Reuses UKB-PPP protein measurements (no de-novo measurement) but performs a genuine HLA-region pQTL scan with fine-mapping; borderline, overlaps UKB-PPP.

snippet: vQTL association studies are performed on plasma levels of 1463 proteins in 52,363 UK Biobank participants. A set of 677 independent vQTLs are identified across 568 proteins.
notes: Borderline: new genome-wide variance-QTL (vQTL) + GEI discovery on protein levels (pGWAS-adjacent). Protein measurements reused from UKB-PPP (Olink Explore), not measured de novo.

Study_population: COPDGene, 3173 Non-Hispanic White + African American; replication SPIROMICS
snippet: we applied sparse multiple canonical correlation network analysis ... to 4,776 proteins assayed on the SomaScan v4.0 platform ... We next performed a genome-wide association study (GWAS) to identify variants associated with the NetSHy scores, or network quantitative trait loci (nQTLs).
notes: De-novo SomaScan measurement in COPDGene; GWAS is on protein-network summary scores (nQTL) rather than standard per-protein pQTL, hence borderline.

Platform: Olink and mass spectrometry (UKB-PPP + MS cohorts)
Study_population: UK Biobank proteomics (48,594), European; replication Olink + MS cohorts
N_pQTL_total: 138 proteins with non-linear effects (123 non-linear covariate; 15 dominance/epistasis)
snippet: Applying this method to the UK Biobank proteomics cohort of 48,594 individuals, we identified 138 proteins that were regulated by non-linear effects, including ... genetic dominance and epistasis (15).
notes: Genome-wide discovery of non-linear/dominance/epistasis pQTL effects, but on reused UKB-PPP (and MS) measurements rather than de-novo measurement; pGWAS-adjacent novel-QTL-type, hence borderline. Likely overlaps UKB-PPP/Sun 2023.

snippet: By taking advantage of the genotype data from The Cancer Genome Atlas and protein expression data from The Cancer Proteome Atlas, we characterized the effects of genetic variants on protein expression across 31 cancer types and identified approximately 100,000 protein quantitative trait loci (pQTL).
notes: Borderline: genome-wide tissue pQTL discovery (~100k) on RPPA (affinity-based) — in-scope tissue proteogenomics. Protein measurements reused from TCPA/TCGA, not measured de novo; database portal (GPIP) also released.

Study_population: UK Biobank (discovery, up to 221,062) + Estonian Biobank (replication); MoBa offspring
snippet: GWAS scans in the UK Biobank for more than 60 complex traits and over 2,400 protein levels contrasting maternal and paternal effects identified over 30 novel POEs.
notes: Parent-of-origin GWAS on >2,400 UKB-PPP protein levels (and 60 complex traits); genome-wide POE pQTL discovery but reuses UKB-PPP Olink measurements (no de-novo measurement); method/POE-focused - borderline.

snippet: Integrating AD genome-wide association studies and CSF protein quantitative trait loci (pQTLs) identified CRIP1 as a candidate causal protein for AD.
notes: De-novo brain/CSF proteome generated (118 brain, 1,104 CSF); ambiguous whether CSF pQTLs were newly mapped here or pulled from existing studies. Borderline (proteogenomics with plausible de-novo CSF pQTL); preprint (no PMID).
